fields.implementation_summary: Review follow-up reopened after finding a ticket-viewer workspace-label race: the route-level list_workspaces hydration writes workspace_label without request sequencing, so a slow previous workspace response can overwrite the current header label after navigation. Follow-up will guard label hydration and keep canonical workspace ids for routing and requests.
fields.title: [ticket-http][ticket-viewer] Introduce collision-safe public workspace identity
fields.validation_summary: Review finding on 2026-05-22: ticket-viewer route-level workspace label fetch can apply stale results after workspace switches. Required follow-up: add a stale-response guard and validate the mixed-workspace viewer route flow again.

fields.acceptance_criteria: Public workspace identifiers remain reversible under duplicate basenames; list/detail/history/files/asset flows stay stable for both colliding workspaces; ambiguous legacy labels fail typedly or map through an explicit compatibility path; specs and tests document identifier and display-label behavior; duplicate-basename fixtures prove registry, HTTP, and any affected viewer routing do not silently shadow one workspace
fields.implementation_summary: Added a request-sequencing guard around the ticket-viewer route-level workspace-label hydration so stale list_workspaces responses cannot overwrite the current header label after navigation. Canonical workspace ids remain the routing and request keys; only the human-readable header label is refreshed from the workspace listing response.
fields.title: [ticket-http][ticket-viewer] Introduce collision-safe public workspace identity
fields.validation_summary: Validation on 2026-05-22: cargo check --manifest-path memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/Cargo.toml passed. Targeted browser validation via npm run test:e2e:release -- mixed-workspace-root-route.spec.ts partially passed: the display-label route case passed, but a separate mixed-workspace history/file-actions case timed out waiting for /api/tickets/<id>/history (2 passed, 1 failed, 1 skipped).

fields.acceptance_criteria: Public workspace identifiers remain reversible under duplicate basenames; list/detail/history/files/asset flows stay stable for both colliding workspaces; ambiguous legacy labels fail typedly or map through an explicit compatibility path; specs and tests document identifier and display-label behavior; duplicate-basename fixtures prove registry, HTTP, and any affected viewer routing do not silently shadow one workspace
fields.implementation_summary: Added a request-sequencing guard around the ticket-viewer route-level workspace-label hydration so stale list_workspaces responses cannot overwrite the current header label after navigation. Canonical workspace ids remain the routing and request keys; only the human-readable header label is refreshed from the workspace listing response. Hardened the mixed-workspace release coverage to compare parsed workspace query params semantically and re-enabled the owning-workspace asset request assertion.
fields.title: [ticket-http][ticket-viewer] Introduce collision-safe public workspace identity
fields.validation_summary: Validation on 2026-05-22: cargo check --manifest-path memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/Cargo.toml passed. npm run test:e2e:release -- mixed-workspace-root-route.spec.ts passed with 4/4 tests green, covering history, files, display-label rendering, content swapping, and owning-workspace asset requests for mixed-workspace tickets.

fields.validation_summary: Validation on 2026-05-22: cargo check --manifest-path memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/Cargo.toml passed. npm run test:e2e:release -- mixed-workspace-root-route.spec.ts passed with 4/4 tests green, covering history, files, display-label rendering, content swapping, and owning-workspace asset requests for mixed-workspace tickets.

fields.acceptance_criteria: Public workspace identifiers remain reversible under duplicate basenames; list/detail/history/files/asset flows stay stable for both colliding workspaces; ambiguous legacy labels fail typedly or map through an explicit compatibility path; specs and tests document identifier and display-label behavior; duplicate-basename fixtures prove registry, HTTP, and any affected viewer routing do not silently shadow one workspace
fields.implementation_summary: ticket-http now rejects display labels as public workspace ids and routes all workspace query params through a shared typed resolver so canonical ids stay stable across list, detail, history, files, asset, graph, batch, schema, and mutation flows. ticket-viewer continues rendering human-readable labels in the header while keeping canonical workspace ids in routes and requests. The release fixture now seeds two child workspaces with the same basename and proves both shared-- canonical ids stay distinct through list, detail, history, files, and asset follow-ups.
fields.title: [ticket-http][ticket-viewer] Introduce collision-safe public workspace identity
fields.validation_summary: Validation on 2026-05-22: cargo test -p ticket-http unique_display_workspace_label_returns_typed_bad_request, legacy_workspace_label_collision_returns_typed_bad_request, list_rejects_synthetic_or_unknown_public_workspace_identifiers, descendant_workspaces_use_workspace_root_name, duplicate_basename_workspaces_receive_distinct_public_ids, and duplicate_basename_workspaces_keep_followups_distinct all passed. npm run test:e2e:release -- mixed-workspace-root-route.spec.ts passed with 5/5 tests green. npm run test:e2e:release:headed -- mixed-workspace-root-route.spec.ts --grep "colliding workspace routes keep list and follow-up requests pinned to canonical ids" passed in headed Chromium.
